plan_metadata.name: Feature LocalDebug bot with existing AAD

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" dialog.
Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu.
Click on the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface at coordinates (288, 101).
Click the "Default folder" field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects".
Press the F1 key in the Visual Studio Code Explorer view with the "VS.CUSE_APP_UTKTH" workspace open to trigger the Command Palette.
Type text: 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input box at the top of the interface.
Press the Enter key to select the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type text 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' into the Visual Studio Code command palette search box at the top of the interface.
Press Enter to select the highlighted "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 Toolkit permission dialog within the Visual Studio Code interface to authenticate your account.
Type 'test04@atktesting05.onmicrosoft.com' into the "Email or phone" input field on the Microsoft account sign-in page in a Chrome browser.
Press the Enter key after typing the email address in the Microsoft login "Sign in" input field to activate the "Next" button.
Type the text ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the Password input field on the Microsoft login page for the account test04@atktesting05.onmicrosoft.com.
Press the Enter key in the "Enter password" text input field on the Microsoft login page to submit the password for the account being signed in.
Click the browser window’s close button (top right corner) in Chrome to close the “M365 Account - Sign In” tab after successful Visual Studio Code authentication.
Click inside the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field on the Microsoft sign-in page within a Chrome browser window.
Enter "test04@atktesting05.onmicrosoft.com" into the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field on the Microsoft Sign in page within a Chrome browser window.
Press the Enter key while the focus is on the email input field of the Microsoft sign-in page in a Chrome browser to submit the entered email address.
Type text ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the Password input field on the Microsoft login page for the account test04@atktesting05.onmicrosoft.com.
Press Enter after entering the password in the Microsoft login "Enter password" field to submit credentials on the Microsoft sign-in page in Chrome.
Click the "Yes" button on the Microsoft "Stay signed in?" dialog within the Microsoft online login page, confirming the user's choice to remain signed in.
Click the "Add" button in the popup for the app "vscuse_app_fja6Dlocal" within the Microsoft Teams web interface to initiate adding the app.
Click the "Open" button in the "Added successfully!" dialog for the app "vscuse_app_fja6Dlocal" within the Microsoft Teams web interface to proceed with app setup.
Click inside the "Type a message" input box at the bottom of a Microsoft Teams chat window to focus the message composer.
Type the text "${{var:test_data:test_######}}" into the message input box at the bottom of the Microsoft Teams chat interface within the "vscuse_app_fja6Dlocal" channel.
Press the Enter key to send the message "test" in the chat input box of the Microsoft Teams web application (in the vscuse_app_fja6Dlocal chat).
@assertion ${{var:test_data}} is visible
